skill.name: specdd-task
skill.description: Use when Agent needs to find, add, interpret, organize, or update SpecDD `Tasks` entries.
skill.summary: Use when Agent needs to find, add, interpret, organize, or update SpecDD `Tasks` entries.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: The static external-command and weak-cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks and task status markers in SKILL.md. No shell execution, cryptographic operation, network access, environment access, or prompt injection attempt was found. The remaining risk is low because the skill directs agents to read and update local SpecDD task files.

low_findings.title: Local Spec File Read and Edit Scope
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to read SpecDD bootstrap and governing spec files, then update task status in the targeted spec. This is expected behavior for a task-management skill, but users should apply it only in trusted project workspaces.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file access and task update workflow is directly described in SKILL.md. The behavior is legitimate for the stated purpose, and no evidence of malicious file access was found.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptographic algorithm matches are Markdown formatting and task status examples, not executable code. No Ruby, shell invocation, or cryptographic API usage appears in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain Markdown text, inline code spans, file paths, and checklist symbols. There is no executable script or command syntax in the skill package.

content.value_statement: SpecDD projects need task updates that respect active specs and write authority. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through scoped task review, status changes, and reporting.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when a project is already configured for SpecDD task handling.
Guides agents to read the active bootstrap chain before task work.
Resolves governing specs before planning, editing, reviewing, or reporting.
Supports adding local, actionable tasks that respect inherited constraints.
Defines task status markers for complete, blocked, uncertain, and skipped work.
Structures final reports with task identifiers, status changes, and verification.
limitations: Works only in projects that already contain a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file.
Does not adopt SpecDD into a new project without explicit operator direction.
Does not grant write authority unless a governing spec already provides it.
Does not complete unrelated tasks outside the requested scope.

use_cases.title: Update a Feature Task
use_cases.description: Mark a targeted SpecDD task complete only after implementation and checks are finished.

use_cases.title: Organize Project Work
use_cases.description: Add concise, local tasks to the correct spec while preserving inherited constraints.

use_cases.title: Review Task Status
use_cases.description: Report blocked, uncertain, skipped, and completed work with exact task references.

prompt_templates.title: Find Relevant Tasks
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for task discovery in a SpecDD project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-task to find the SpecDD tasks that apply to this requested change. Report the governing spec path and relevant task text.

prompt_templates.title: Add Scoped Tasks
prompt_templates.scenario: Create implementation tasks without changing unrelated specs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-task to add local, actionable tasks for this feature. Respect the active bootstrap chain and confirm the nearest write authority first.

prompt_templates.title: Update Task Status
prompt_templates.scenario: Change task markers after implementation and checks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-task to update the targeted task status after verifying the completed work. Include the task id or exact task text in your report.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Task Chain
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across inherited SpecDD constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-task to resolve the effective spec chain for these changed files. Identify blocked or uncertain tasks and explain which governing specs control them.

output_examples.input: Find tasks for the payment retry change.
output_examples.output: The report names the applicable spec, lists the matching task text, and notes which bootstrap files governed the lookup.

output_examples.input: Mark the migration task complete after tests pass.
output_examples.output: The response confirms the completed task, summarizes the verification, and leaves unrelated tasks unchanged.

output_examples.input: Add tasks for the new notification flow.
output_examples.output: The result adds concise implementation tasks to the authorized local spec and explains any inherited constraints.

best_practices: Use the skill only after confirming the project has .specdd/bootstrap.md.
Resolve the effective spec chain before editing task status or adding tasks.
Report exact task text, status changes, verification, and unresolved work.
anti_patterns: Do not use task entries to override Must, Must not, Forbids, or write authority.
Do not infer write authority from nearby filenames or informal project conventions.
Do not complete unrelated tasks while handling the requested task.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides task handling in SpecDD projects, including discovery, edits, status updates, and reporting.

faq.question: Does it work in non-SpecDD projects?
faq.answer: No. It requires a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file before the workflow should continue.

faq.question: Can it add SpecDD to a project?
faq.answer: No. It tells agents to use a separate adoption workflow only when the operator explicitly asks.

faq.question: Can it run commands?
faq.answer: No command execution is defined in the skill. Static command findings were Markdown false positives.

faq.question: What files can it affect?
faq.answer: It is intended for .sdd task-bearing specs and related SpecDD bootstrap or governing spec files.

faq.question: How should results be reported?
faq.answer: Reports should include task ids or exact task text, status changes, verification, and remaining blocked or uncertain tasks.
